generated_oml:
instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Thermal incubator system consisting of a styrofoam box (0.03 m³ volume), heatbed (214×214×3 mm), fan (airflow 0.02 m³/s), three DS18S20 temperature sensors, Raspberry Pi 4 Model B controller, PCB with MOSFETs and optocouplers, and power supply. The system regulates internal temperature while minimizing environmental impact through insulation."
    base:contains StyrofoamBox, Heatbed, Fan, DS18S20SensorA, DS18S20SensorB, DS18S20SensorC, RaspberryPiController, PCB, PowerSupply
]

instance StyrofoamBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box with 0.03 m³ volume for thermal insulation in incubator system."
]

instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Heatbed (214×214×3 mm) controlled via MOSFETs and optocouplers for 4A current handling."
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan (12V/24V operation) actuated through Raspberry Pi GPIO pins using custom PCB for airflow 0.02 m³/s."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B controller for executing digital twin components and controlling physical actuators."
]

instance PCB : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "PCB with MOSFETs and optocouplers for electrical safety and current handling in actuation circuits."
]

instance PowerSupply : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Power supply unit for the thermal incubator system."
]

instance DS18S20SensorA : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring temperatures from -55°C to +125°C with ±0.5°C accuracy between -10°C to +85°C. Unique 64-bit serial code."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DS18S20TemperatureDataA
]

instance DS18S20SensorB : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring temperatures from -55°C to +125°C with ±0.5°C accuracy between -10°C to +85°C. Unique 64-bit serial code."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DS18S20TemperatureDataB
]

instance DS18S20SensorC : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring temperatures from -55°C to +125°C with ±0.5°C accuracy between -10°C to +85°C. Unique 64-bit serial code."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DS18S20TemperatureDataC
]

instance DS18S20TemperatureDataA : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20SensorA
]

instance DS18S20TemperatureDataB : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20SensorB
]

instance DS18S20TemperatureDataC : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DS18S20SensorC
]

instance DS18S20RawTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from DS18S20 sensors transmitted via 1-Wire bus."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DS18S20TemperatureDataA, DS18S20TemperatureDataB, DS18S20TemperatureDataC
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ScipyCalibrationEnabler
]

instance ModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic thermal model with 2 parameters assuming all power energy transfers to air."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ScipyCalibrationEnabler, TemperaturePredictionService
]

instance ModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic thermal model with 4 parameters (C_heater, C_air, G_heater, G_box) accounting for heatbed thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ScipyCalibrationEnabler, TemperaturePredictionService
]

instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message broker for communication between physical sensors and digital twin components via RabbitMQ server on Raspberry Pi."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, FaultDetectionService, TemperaturePredictionService
]

instance ScipyCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization library for calibrating thermal models using non-linear least squares solver."
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService
]

instance PythonImplementationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python programming language used for implementing digital twin components and services."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, FaultDetectionService, CalibrationService, TemperaturePredictionService
]

instance GitHubRepositoryEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "GitHub for open-source repository hosting of digital twin code and documentation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelA, ModelB, CalibrationService
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring for safety compliance of thermal incubator system temperatures."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyMonitoringInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Fault detection via state validation of thermal system parameters."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Calibration of physical parameters using experimental data from DS18S20 sensors."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelCalibrationInsight
]

instance TemperaturePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive capabilities for thermal behavior under varying operating conditions using Model A and Model B."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detects temperature deviations from setpoints for safety compliance."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identifies faults in thermal system through state validation."
]

instance ModelCalibrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides calibrated parameters for thermal models based on experimental data."
]

instance ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicts thermal behavior under varying operating conditions for optimization."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Controller commands (e.g., heatbed on/off, fan speed control) are sent via RabbitMQ to the low-level driver, which executes actions through the PCB. The bang-bang controller uses parameters LL (lower limit), UL (upper limit), H (heating duration), and C (waiting duration) to regulate temperature based on sensor feedback."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ server on Raspberry Pi coordinates communication between physical components (sensors/actuators via low-level driver) and digital twin components (controller, simulation models). Information flows through message queues with the controller processing sensor data and issuing actuation commands."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin integrates models after system deployment, covering the 'as operated' phase of the physical twin. Models are maintained for ongoing operational analysis rather than discarded post-deployment."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity validated through uniform temperature experiments (three-sensor placement tests) and calibration via least-squares optimization. Model B demonstrated superior accuracy for thermal inertia prediction due to inclusion of heatbed heat accumulation dynamics, with residual error minimized through Scipy optimization."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker used for communication between physical and virtual components. Data transmission occurs via AMQP protocol over local network on Raspberry Pi, with no external network dependencies specified."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "All digital twin components (RabbitMQ server, controller, low-level driver, simulation models) are hosted on Raspberry Pi 4 Model B. The system operates as an embedded solution with no cloud or remote deployment."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Low-level driver implements a safe protocol to maintain device safety. PCB design uses optocouplers for electrical decoupling between control signals and high-current driving circuits, preventing direct Raspberry Pi exposure to hazardous currents."
]
